totally.en: to a complete or entire extent

ex.en: I totally agree with your plan.
ex.ko: 나는 네 계획에 완전히 동의해.

tourism.en: the business of providing services such as tours and accommodation to visitors

ex.en: Tourism is the main source of income on the island.
ex.ko: 관광업은 그 섬의 주요 수입원이다.

tournament.en: a sports competition in which players compete against each other until one winner remains

ex.en: Our school is hosting a chess tournament this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리 학교는 이번 주말에 체스 대회를 연다.

trace.en: find or discover something by following evidence back to its origin

ex.en: Police traced the call to an address downtown.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 전화를 시내의 한 주소지로 추적했다.

trace.en: a very small sign that something existed or happened

ex.en: There was no trace of the missing hikers.
ex.ko: 실종된 등산객들의 흔적은 전혀 없었다.

tracksuit.en: a loose, comfortable set of clothes worn for exercising or relaxing

ex.en: He wore a tracksuit to the gym.
ex.ko: 그는 운동복을 입고 체육관에 갔다.

ex.en: Traditionally, the whole family gathers for dinner on Sundays.
ex.ko: 전통적으로 일요일마다 온 가족이 모여 저녁을 먹는다.

traffic jam.en: a long line of vehicles that cannot move or moves very slowly

ex.en: We were stuck in a traffic jam for an hour.
ex.ko: 우리는 한 시간 동안 교통 체증에 갇혀 있었다.

tragedy.en: an event that causes great sadness, suffering, or loss

ex.en: The fire was a terrible tragedy for the whole town.
ex.ko: 그 화재는 마을 전체에게 끔찍한 비극이었다.

tragedy.en: a serious play in which the main character suffers a downfall or death

ex.en: Shakespeare wrote many famous tragedies.
ex.ko: 셰익스피어는 많은 유명한 비극을 썼다.

tragic.en: causing or involving great sadness, suffering, or loss

ex.en: Her death was a tragic accident.
ex.ko: 그녀의 죽음은 비극적인 사고였다.

trail.en: to be behind someone or something in a race, competition, or contest

ex.en: Our team was trailing by ten points at halftime.
ex.ko: 우리 팀은 전반전에 10점 차로 뒤처지고 있었다.

trail.en: a rough path through the countryside or hills

ex.en: We followed a trail through the forest.
ex.ko: 우리는 숲 속의 오솔길을 따라갔다.

transfer.en: to move something from one place, person, or account to another

ex.en: Please transfer the money to my account.
ex.ko: 제 계좌로 돈을 이체해 주세요.

transfer.en: the act of changing from one bus, train, or flight to another during a journey

ex.en: You can get a free transfer to the subway with this ticket.
ex.ko: 이 표로 지하철로 무료 환승을 할 수 있다.

transfer.en: to move an employee to a different department, branch, or location

ex.en: The company transferred him to the Tokyo office.
ex.ko: 회사는 그를 도쿄 지사로 전근시켰다.

transform.en: to change completely the form, appearance, or nature of something

ex.en: The old warehouse was transformed into a museum.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 창고는 박물관으로 완전히 탈바꿈했다.

transformation.en: a complete change in the form, appearance, or nature of something

ex.en: The city has undergone a huge transformation in the last decade.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 지난 10년간 엄청난 변화를 겪었다.

transitional.en: relating to a period of changing from one state or condition to another

ex.en: It's a transitional period for the whole industry.
ex.ko: 그 산업 전체에게 지금은 과도기이다.

translate.en: to change words from one language into another

ex.en: Can you translate this letter into English?
ex.ko: 이 편지를 영어로 번역해 줄 수 있어?

translate.en: to result in or be equivalent to something else

ex.en: Good reviews don't always translate into higher sales.
ex.ko: 좋은 후기가 항상 더 높은 매출로 이어지는 것은 아니다.

transport.en: to carry people or goods from one place to another

ex.en: The goods are transported by truck.
ex.ko: 그 물품들은 트럭으로 운송된다.

transport.en: a system or means of traveling or moving things from one place to another

ex.en: Public transport in this city is very reliable.
ex.ko: 이 도시의 대중교통은 매우 믿을 만하다.

transportation.en: the system of vehicles used for moving people or goods from place to place

ex.en: Public transportation here is cheap and fast.
ex.ko: 이곳의 대중교통은 저렴하고 빠르다.

trash.en: things that are thrown away because they are no longer wanted

ex.en: Please take out the trash before you leave.
ex.ko: 나가기 전에 쓰레기 좀 내다 버려 줘.

trash.en: to throw something away because it is no longer wanted

ex.en: She trashed all her old notebooks.
ex.ko: 그녀는 낡은 공책들을 전부 버렸다.

trash.en: to criticize someone or something very harshly

ex.en: Critics trashed the director's new movie.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 감독의 새 영화를 혹평했다.

travel agent.en: a person whose job is to arrange trips, flights, and hotels for customers

ex.en: We booked our vacation through a travel agent.
ex.ko: 우리는 여행사 직원을 통해 휴가를 예약했다.

treat.en: to behave toward someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: He treats all his employees with respect.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 직원을 존중하며 대한다.

treat.en: to give medical care to a person or an illness

ex.en: The doctor treated her for a broken arm.
ex.ko: 의사는 그녀의 부러진 팔을 치료했다.

treat.en: something special that gives someone pleasure, especially food or an outing

ex.en: Ice cream is a nice treat on a hot day.
ex.ko: 더운 날엔 아이스크림이 좋은 간식이다.

treat.en: to pay for something enjoyable for someone else

ex.en: Let me treat you to dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 저녁은 내가 대접할게.

treatment.en: medical care given to cure an illness or injury

ex.en: She is receiving treatment for cancer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 암 치료를 받고 있다.

treatment.en: the way a person or group is dealt with by others

ex.en: Workers complained about unfair treatment.
ex.ko: 근로자들은 부당한 대우에 대해 불만을 제기했다.

tremble.en: to shake slightly, especially because of fear, cold, or excitement

ex.en: Her hands trembled as she opened the letter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 편지를 열면서 손이 떨렸다.

tremendous.en: extremely large in amount, size, or degree

ex.en: There was a tremendous amount of traffic today.
ex.ko: 오늘은 차가 엄청나게 막혔다.

ex.en: She did a tremendous job on the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트에서 대단한 일을 해냈다.

trend.en: a general direction in which something is changing or developing

ex.en: There's a growing trend toward remote work.
ex.ko: 재택근무로 가는 추세가 점점 커지고 있다.

trend.en: a style or activity that is currently popular

ex.en: Short hair is a big trend this year.
ex.ko: 짧은 머리가 올해 큰 유행이다.

trigger.cue: 총을 쏠 때 손가락으로 당기는 부분
trigger.en: the small lever on a gun that you pull to fire it

ex.en: He pulled the trigger by accident.
ex.ko: 그는 실수로 방아쇠를 당겼다.

trigger.en: to cause something to start happening suddenly

ex.en: The comment triggered a huge argument.
ex.ko: 그 발언이 큰 말다툼을 촉발시켰다.

trim.en: to cut a small amount off something to make it neater

ex.en: I need to get my hair trimmed.
ex.ko: 나는 머리를 다듬어야 한다.

trim.en: slim and fit, with no extra fat

ex.en: He looked trim after months at the gym.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 달간 헬스장에 다닌 후 몸이 날씬해 보였다.

trim.en: to reduce the amount of something, such as costs

ex.en: The company had to trim its budget this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 예산을 삭감해야 했다.

triumph.en: a great success or victory after a difficult struggle

ex.en: Winning the championship was a personal triumph for her.
ex.ko: 우승은 그녀에게 개인적인 큰 성공이었다.

triumph.en: to succeed or win after a difficult struggle

ex.en: She triumphed over many challenges to become a doctor.
ex.ko: 그녀는 많은 어려움을 이겨내고 의사가 되었다.

tropical.en: relating to or typical of the hot, humid regions near the equator

ex.en: They spent their honeymoon on a tropical island.
ex.ko: 그들은 열대 섬에서 신혼여행을 보냈다.

trumpet.en: a brass musical instrument played by blowing into it

ex.en: He plays the trumpet in the school band.
ex.ko: 그는 학교 밴드에서 트럼펫을 연주한다.

trumpet.en: to announce something loudly and proudly

ex.en: The company trumpeted its record profits.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 사상 최고 이익을 대대적으로 알렸다.

trustworthy.en: able to be trusted or relied on

ex.en: She is a trustworthy friend who keeps her promises.
ex.ko: 그녀는 약속을 지키는 신뢰할 수 있는 친구다.

tumble.en: to fall down suddenly, often rolling over

ex.en: He tumbled down the stairs.
ex.ko: 그는 계단에서 굴러떨어졌다.

tumble.en: to decrease suddenly and by a large amount

ex.en: Stock prices tumbled after the announcement.
ex.ko: 그 발표 후 주가가 급락했다.

tuna.en: a large sea fish that is often eaten canned or as sushi

ex.en: She made a tuna sandwich for lunch.
ex.ko: 그녀는 점심으로 참치 샌드위치를 만들었다.

turbulence.en: rough, uneven movement of air that causes an aircraft to shake

ex.en: The plane shook because of severe turbulence.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 심한 난기류 때문에 흔들렸다.

turbulence.en: a state of great confusion, disorder, or change

ex.en: The country went through years of political turbulence.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 수년간 정치적 격동을 겪었다.

turning.en: the act or point of changing direction or course

ex.en: Getting that job was a turning point in her life.
ex.ko: 그 일자리를 얻은 것은 그녀 인생의 전환점이었다.

turning.en: a place where a road turns off from another road

ex.en: Take the second turning on the left.
ex.ko: 왼쪽 두 번째 갈림길로 가세요.

turtle.en: a reptile with a hard shell that lives on land or in water

ex.en: The turtle slowly crossed the road.
ex.ko: 그 거북은 천천히 도로를 건넜다.

twin.en: one of two children born to the same mother at the same time

ex.en: My sister and I are twins.
ex.ko: 내 여동생과 나는 쌍둥이다.

twin.en: consisting of two things that are identical or very similar

ex.en: The hotel room had twin beds.
ex.ko: 그 호텔 방에는 트윈 침대(1인용 침대 두 개)가 있었다.

twist.en: to turn or bend something using force

ex.en: She twisted the wet towel to wring out the water.
ex.ko: 그녀는 물기를 짜내려고 젖은 수건을 비틀었다.

twist.en: an unexpected change or development in a story or situation

ex.en: The movie had a surprising twist at the end.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 결말에 놀라운 반전이 있었다.

twist.en: to injure a joint by turning it suddenly and awkwardly

ex.en: He twisted his ankle while running.
ex.ko: 그는 달리다가 발목을 삐었다.

typical.en: having the usual features or qualities of a particular type of thing or person

ex.en: It was a typical rainy day in London.
ex.ko: 런던의 전형적인 비 오는 날이었다.

typically.en: in a way that is usual or expected for a particular type of thing

ex.en: Typically, the meeting starts at nine.
ex.ko: 일반적으로 회의는 9시에 시작한다.

unable.en: not having the ability, skill, time, or means to do something

ex.en: He was unable to attend the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의에 참석할 수 없었다.

unafraid.en: not feeling fear or worry about something

ex.en: She was unafraid to speak her mind.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 생각을 말하는 것을 두려워하지 않았다.

unbelievable.en: so surprising or extreme that it is hard to believe

ex.en: The view from the top was unbelievable.
ex.ko: 정상에서 본 경치는 믿을 수 없을 정도였다.

uncertainty.en: a state of not being sure about something

ex.en: There is a lot of uncertainty about the future of the company.
ex.ko: 그 회사의 미래에 대해 불확실성이 크다.

unclear.en: not clear or easy to understand

ex.en: The instructions were unclear to most students.
ex.ko: 그 지시사항은 대부분의 학생들에게 불명확했다.

ex.en: She burst into uncontrollable laughter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 걷잡을 수 없이 웃음을 터뜨렸다.

uncover.en: to find out something that was previously secret or unknown

ex.en: Reporters uncovered the truth about the scandal.
ex.ko: 기자들은 그 스캔들에 대한 진실을 밝혀냈다.

uncover.en: to remove the cover from something so it can be seen

ex.en: He uncovered the old furniture in the attic.
ex.ko: 그는 다락방에 있던 낡은 가구의 덮개를 벗겼다.

underage.en: not old enough, according to the law, to do something such as drink alcohol

ex.en: It's illegal to sell alcohol to underage customers.
ex.ko: 미성년자에게 술을 파는 것은 불법이다.

undergo.en: to experience something, especially a change or a difficult process

ex.en: She underwent surgery last month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난달 수술을 받았다.

ex.en: The cat hid underneath the bed.
ex.ko: 고양이는 침대 아래에 숨었다.

underpants.en: a piece of underwear worn on the lower part of the body

ex.en: He packed some clean underpants for the trip.
ex.ko: 그는 여행을 위해 깨끗한 속옷을 좀 챙겼다.

undo.en: to reverse an action, returning something to its previous state

ex.en: Press Ctrl+Z to undo your last change.
ex.ko: 마지막 작업을 취소하려면 Ctrl+Z를 누르세요.

undo.en: to unfasten or loosen something such as a button or knot

ex.en: She undid the buttons on her coat.
ex.ko: 그녀는 코트의 단추를 풀었다.

ex.en: The children undressed and got ready for bed.
ex.ko: 아이들은 옷을 벗고 잘 준비를 했다.

unemployed.en: not having a paid job, although able to work

ex.en: He has been unemployed for six months.
ex.ko: 그는 여섯 달째 실업 상태다.

unemployed.en: people, as a group, who do not have jobs

ex.en: The government announced new support for the unemployed.
ex.ko: 정부는 실업자들을 위한 새로운 지원책을 발표했다.

unemployment.en: the state of not having a job, or the number of people without jobs

ex.en: Unemployment rose sharply during the recession.
ex.ko: 불황 동안 실업률이 급격히 올랐다.

ex.en: We had an unexpected visitor last night.
ex.ko: 어젯밤 우리에게 예상치 못한 손님이 왔다.

unexpectedly.en: in a way that was not expected

ex.en: Her grandmother passed away unexpectedly.
ex.ko: 그녀의 할머니는 예상치 못하게 돌아가셨다.

unfairly.en: in a way that is not fair or right

ex.en: He felt he was treated unfairly at work.
ex.ko: 그는 직장에서 부당하게 대우받았다고 느꼈다.

unfit.en: not suitable or good enough for a particular purpose

ex.en: The building was declared unfit for use.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 사용하기에 부적합하다고 선언되었다.

ex.en: He felt unfit after months without exercise.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 달간 운동을 안 했더니 체력이 나빠진 느낌이었다.

unfold.en: to open something that was folded so it becomes flat

ex.en: She unfolded the map on the table.
ex.ko: 그녀는 탁자 위에 지도를 펼쳤다.

unfold.en: to develop or become known gradually

ex.en: We watched the story unfold on the news.
ex.ko: 우리는 뉴스에서 그 이야기가 전개되는 것을 지켜보았다.

unfortunate.en: unlucky, or causing sadness or regret

ex.en: It was an unfortunate accident that could have been avoided.
ex.ko: 그것은 피할 수 있었던 안타까운 사고였다.

unfriendly.en: not kind or pleasant to other people

ex.en: The waiter was rather unfriendly.
ex.ko: 그 종업원은 다소 불친절했다.

unheard.en: never known or done before; extremely unusual

ex.en: A profit that size was unheard of ten years ago.
ex.ko: 10년 전만 해도 그 정도 규모의 이익은 전례가 없었다.

unheard.en: not heard, although possibly not silent

ex.en: Her cries went unheard in the storm.
ex.ko: 폭풍 속에서 그녀의 외침은 들리지 않았다.

unify.en: to bring separate things or people together as one

ex.en: The new leader tried to unify the divided party.
ex.ko: 새 지도자는 분열된 당을 통합하려 했다.

ex.en: He seemed completely uninterested in the conversation.
ex.ko: 그는 그 대화에 전혀 관심이 없어 보였다.

ex.en: It was a long, uninteresting lecture.
ex.ko: 그것은 길고 흥미롭지 않은 강의였다.

union.en: an organization of workers formed to protect their rights and interests

ex.en: She joined the teachers' union.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교원 노동조합에 가입했다.

union.en: the joining together of two or more groups, countries, or things

ex.en: The union of the two companies created a huge corporation.
ex.ko: 두 회사의 통합은 거대한 기업을 만들어냈다.

ex.en: Their union lasted almost fifty years.
ex.ko: 그들의 결혼 생활은 거의 50년 동안 이어졌다.

unique.en: being the only one of its kind; very special or unusual

ex.en: Every snowflake is unique.
ex.ko: 모든 눈송이는 저마다 독특하다.

unite.en: to join together and act as one group

ex.en: The whole town united to help the flood victims.
ex.ko: 온 마을이 홍수 피해자들을 돕기 위해 단결했다.

universe.en: all of space, including every star, planet, and galaxy

ex.en: Scientists still don't know how the universe began.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 아직 우주가 어떻게 시작되었는지 모른다.

unless.en: except if; used to say what will happen if something does not happen

ex.en: We won't go unless it stops raining.
ex.ko: 비가 그치지 않는다면 우리는 가지 않을 것이다.

unlikely.en: not likely to happen or be true

ex.en: It's unlikely that she will change her mind.
ex.ko: 그녀가 마음을 바꿀 가능성은 낮다.

unlucky.cue: 운이 나빠서 안 좋은 일을 겪는

ex.en: He was unlucky enough to lose his wallet twice.
ex.ko: 그는 운이 없게도 지갑을 두 번이나 잃어버렸다.

unpack.en: to take things out of a bag, box, or suitcase

ex.en: I need to unpack my suitcase after the trip.
ex.ko: 여행 후 여행 가방의 짐을 풀어야 한다.

unpredictable.cue: 어떻게 될지 미리 알 수 없는
unpredictable.en: impossible to know or guess in advance

ex.en: The weather here is very unpredictable.
ex.ko: 이곳 날씨는 매우 예측할 수 없다.

unrelated.en: not connected or having no relationship with something else

ex.en: The two incidents were completely unrelated.
ex.ko: 그 두 사건은 서로 전혀 관련이 없었다.

ex.en: His room is always untidy.
ex.ko: 그의 방은 항상 지저분하다.

unusually.en: to a degree that is not usual or normal

ex.en: It was unusually cold for April.
ex.ko: 4월치고는 유난히 추웠다.

ex.en: She gave away her unwanted clothes to charity.
ex.ko: 그녀는 필요 없는 옷들을 자선단체에 기부했다.

ex.en: She stayed home because she felt unwell.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몸이 안 좋아서 집에 있었다.

up-to-date.en: having the most recent information, features, or style

ex.en: This textbook is not up-to-date anymore.
ex.ko: 이 교과서는 더 이상 최신이 아니다.

update.en: new information that adds to or corrects what is already known

ex.en: Give me an update on the project.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트에 대한 최신 소식을 알려줘.

update.en: to make something more modern or add the latest information to it

ex.en: Please update your app to the latest version.
ex.ko: 앱을 최신 버전으로 업데이트해 주세요.

uplifted.en: feeling happier or more hopeful than before

ex.en: I felt uplifted after talking with her.
ex.ko: 그녀와 이야기한 후 기분이 좋아졌다.

upload.en: to send data or files from your device to the internet or another computer

ex.en: She uploaded the photos to her account.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사진들을 자기 계정에 업로드했다.

upper.cue: 두 개 중에 더 위쪽에 있는
upper.en: higher in position than something else

ex.en: Her office is on the upper floor.
ex.ko: 그녀의 사무실은 위층에 있다.

upward.en: moving or pointing toward a higher place

ex.en: Sales have shown an upward trend this year.
ex.ko: 올해 매출은 상승 추세를 보였다.

urge.en: to strongly encourage or advise someone to do something

ex.en: Doctors urge people to get more sleep.
ex.ko: 의사들은 사람들에게 잠을 더 자라고 권고한다.

urge.en: a strong desire to do something

ex.en: She felt an urge to laugh during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 웃고 싶은 충동을 느꼈다.

ex.en: This is an urgent matter that can't wait.
ex.ko: 이것은 미룰 수 없는 긴급한 문제이다.

usage.en: the way something is used, or the amount of it that is used

ex.en: The app shows your daily data usage.
ex.ko: 그 앱은 일일 데이터 사용량을 보여준다.

usage.en: the way words are normally used in a language

ex.en: This dictionary explains modern English usage.
ex.ko: 이 사전은 현대 영어 어법을 설명한다.

used to.en: used to describe something that happened regularly in the past but not now

ex.en: I used to play soccer every weekend.
ex.ko: 나는 예전에 주말마다 축구를 하곤 했다.

used to.en: familiar with something because you have experienced it many times

ex.en: She is used to waking up early.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일찍 일어나는 것에 익숙하다.

useless.en: not useful or helpful; not working properly

ex.en: This old phone is useless now.
ex.ko: 이 낡은 휴대폰은 이제 쓸모없다.

vacancy.en: a job or position that is available because no one is doing it

ex.en: There is a vacancy for a sales manager.
ex.ko: 영업 관리자 공석이 있다.

vacancy.en: an empty room available to rent, especially in a hotel

ex.en: The hotel had no vacancies during the holidays.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 휴가철에 빈방이 없었다.

vague.en: not clearly expressed or not easy to understand

ex.en: His answer was too vague to be useful.
ex.ko: 그의 대답은 너무 모호해서 도움이 되지 않았다.

vain.en: producing no result; useless despite effort

ex.en: They made a vain attempt to save the building.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 건물을 지키려는 헛된 시도를 했다.

vain.en: too proud of your own appearance or achievements

ex.en: He's too vain to wear glasses in public.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 허영심이 많아서 사람들 앞에서 안경을 안 쓴다.

valuable.en: worth a lot of money, or very useful and important

ex.en: This ring is very valuable.
ex.ko: 이 반지는 매우 값진 것이다.

van.cue: 짐이나 승객을 싣는 상자 모양 차
van.en: a vehicle with a box-shaped body used to carry goods or passengers

ex.en: They loaded the boxes into the van.
ex.ko: 그들은 상자들을 밴에 실었다.

variety.en: the quality of not being the same, or of including many different things

ex.en: Adding variety to your diet is important.
ex.ko: 식단에 다양성을 더하는 것은 중요하다.

variety.en: a number of different types of the same general thing

ex.en: The store sells a variety of fresh fruits.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 다양한 신선한 과일을 판다.

various.en: of several different types or kinds

ex.en: The book covers various topics in science.
ex.ko: 그 책은 과학의 다양한 주제를 다룬다.

vary.en: to be different or to change according to the situation

ex.en: Prices vary depending on the season.
ex.ko: 가격은 계절에 따라 달라진다.

vast.en: extremely large in area, size, or amount

ex.en: The desert stretched over a vast area.
ex.ko: 그 사막은 광대한 지역에 걸쳐 있었다.

vegetarian.en: a person who does not eat meat or fish

ex.en: My sister has been a vegetarian for ten years.
ex.ko: 내 여동생은 10년째 채식주의자다.

ex.en: The restaurant offers several vegetarian dishes.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 몇 가지 채식 요리를 제공한다.

vehicle.en: a machine, usually with wheels and an engine, used to transport people or goods

ex.en: Only authorized vehicles may enter this area.
ex.ko: 허가된 차량만 이 구역에 들어올 수 있다.

vein.en: one of the tubes that carries blood from the body back to the heart

ex.en: The nurse found a vein to draw blood from.
ex.ko: 간호사는 채혈할 정맥을 찾았다.

vessel.en: a tube in the body through which blood flows

ex.en: A blood vessel in his leg was blocked.
ex.ko: 그의 다리 혈관 하나가 막혀 있었다.

ex.en: The fishing vessel returned to port at dawn.
ex.ko: 그 어선은 새벽에 항구로 돌아왔다.

vet.en: a doctor who treats sick or injured animals

ex.en: We took the dog to the vet.
ex.ko: 우리는 개를 데리고 수의사에게 갔다.

vet.en: to check someone or something carefully before approving them

ex.en: The company vets all new employees carefully.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 모든 신입사원을 꼼꼼히 검증한다.

via.en: traveling through a place, or using a particular method

ex.en: We flew to Paris via London.
ex.ko: 우리는 런던을 경유해서 파리로 갔다.

vice.en: an immoral or bad habit or type of behavior

ex.en: Smoking is his only vice.
ex.ko: 흡연은 그의 유일한 나쁜 습관이다.

vice president.en: the person who ranks immediately below a president in a government or company

ex.en: She was elected vice president of the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 회사의 부사장으로 선출되었다.

victim.en: a person who has been hurt, harmed, or killed by someone or something

ex.en: The victim was taken to the hospital.
ex.ko: 그 피해자는 병원으로 이송되었다.

victory.en: success in a battle, game, or competition

ex.en: The team celebrated their victory with the fans.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 팬들과 함께 승리를 축하했다.

video clip.en: a short piece of video, often shared online

ex.en: She posted a funny video clip on social media.
ex.ko: 그녀는 SNS에 재미있는 동영상 클립을 올렸다.

viewpoint.en: a particular way of thinking about or judging something

ex.en: From my viewpoint, the plan makes no sense.
ex.ko: 내 관점에서는 그 계획이 말이 안 된다.

violence.en: behavior that is intended to hurt or kill people

ex.en: The movie contains scenes of violence.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 폭력 장면이 나온다.

violently.en: in a way that involves great force or aggression

ex.en: The two men argued violently.
ex.ko: 그 두 남자는 격렬하게 다퉜다.

virtual.en: existing only in a computer program or online, not physically

ex.en: They held a virtual meeting instead of meeting in person.
ex.ko: 그들은 직접 만나는 대신 화상 회의를 열었다.

virtual.en: almost like something in every way, though not officially

ex.en: The company has a virtual monopoly on the market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 사실상 시장을 독점하고 있다.

virus.en: a very small living thing that causes disease and can spread from person to person

ex.en: The virus spread quickly through the school.
ex.ko: 그 바이러스는 학교 전체에 빠르게 퍼졌다.

virus.en: a computer program that can damage files or spread to other computers

ex.en: Her laptop was infected with a virus.
ex.ko: 그녀의 노트북이 바이러스에 감염되었다.

visa.en: an official document or stamp that allows you to enter a foreign country

ex.en: You need a visa to work in that country.
ex.ko: 그 나라에서 일하려면 비자가 필요하다.

ex.en: The mountain was visible from our hotel window.
ex.ko: 그 산은 우리 호텔 창문에서 보였다.

ex.en: His vision has gotten worse with age.
ex.ko: 그의 시력은 나이가 들면서 나빠졌다.

vision.en: an idea or picture of what something could be like in the future

ex.en: The company has a clear vision for the next ten years.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 향후 10년에 대한 명확한 비전이 있다.

ex.en: The presentation used many visual aids.
ex.ko: 그 발표는 여러 시각 자료를 사용했다.

visually.en: in a way that relates to sight or appearance

ex.en: The design is visually appealing.
ex.ko: 그 디자인은 시각적으로 매력적이다.

vivid.en: producing very clear and detailed images in the mind, or having strong bright colors

ex.en: She has a vivid memory of her first day at school.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학교 첫날에 대한 생생한 기억이 있다.

vividly.en: in a way that produces clear, strong, and detailed images

ex.en: He described the accident vividly.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고를 생생하게 묘사했다.

volcano.en: a mountain with an opening through which hot melted rock and gas can escape

ex.en: The volcano erupted after being quiet for years.
ex.ko: 그 화산은 수년간 잠잠하다가 분화했다.

ex.en: Could you turn down the volume, please?
ex.ko: 볼륨 좀 낮춰 줄래?

volume.en: the amount of space that something occupies, or a large amount of something

ex.en: The tank holds a large volume of water.
ex.ko: 그 탱크는 많은 양의 물을 담을 수 있다.

volunteer.en: a person who does a job or helps other people without being paid

ex.en: She works as a volunteer at the animal shelter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동물 보호소에서 자원봉사자로 일한다.

volunteer.en: to offer to do something without being asked or paid

ex.en: He volunteered to clean up after the party.
ex.ko: 그는 파티가 끝난 후 청소를 자원했다.

vowel.cue: a, e, i, o, u 같은 소리·글자
vowel.en: a speech sound made without blocking the air, such as a, e, i, o, u

ex.en: "A", "e", and "i" are all vowels.
ex.ko: 'a', 'e', 'i'는 모두 모음이다.

voyage.en: a long journey, especially by sea or in space

ex.en: The ship set out on a voyage across the Pacific.
ex.ko: 그 배는 태평양을 가로지르는 항해를 시작했다.

wagon.en: a vehicle with wheels, pulled by animals, used to carry goods

ex.en: Settlers crossed the country in covered wagons.
ex.ko: 이주민들은 덮개 있는 마차를 타고 나라를 가로질렀다.

wagon.en: a car with a long body and extra space at the back for luggage

ex.en: They packed the wagon for the family road trip.
ex.ko: 그들은 가족 여행을 위해 왜건 차에 짐을 실었다.

ward.en: a section of a hospital where patients with similar needs stay

ex.en: Her father is in the intensive care ward.
ex.ko: 그녀의 아버지는 중환자 병동에 있다.

ward.en: a child or young person who is legally under the protection of an adult or court

ex.en: The judge appointed a guardian for the young ward.
ex.ko: 판사는 그 어린 피후견인을 위해 후견인을 지정했다.

wardrobe.en: a tall piece of furniture in which you hang your clothes

ex.en: He hung his suit in the wardrobe.
ex.ko: 그는 옷장에 정장을 걸었다.

wardrobe.en: all the clothes that a person owns

ex.en: She updated her wardrobe for the new season.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 계절을 맞아 옷을 새로 장만했다.

warmly.en: in a friendly, kind, or affectionate way

ex.en: They welcomed us warmly at the door.
ex.ko: 그들은 문에서 우리를 따뜻하게 맞아 주었다.

warmth.en: a moderate degree of heat, or a friendly and kind feeling

ex.en: She greeted everyone with warmth and a smile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 따뜻함과 미소로 모두를 맞이했다.

warn.en: to tell someone about a possible danger or problem in advance

ex.en: The sign warns visitors about the cliff edge.
ex.ko: 그 표지판은 방문객들에게 절벽 가장자리를 경고한다.

warning.en: a statement or sign that tells people about a possible danger

ex.en: The teacher gave him a warning about being late.
ex.ko: 선생님은 그에게 지각에 대해 경고했다.

warranty.en: a written promise from a company to repair or replace a product if it breaks

ex.en: The laptop comes with a two-year warranty.
ex.ko: 그 노트북은 2년 품질보증서가 딸려 있다.

warrior.en: a person who fights in battles, especially in the past

ex.en: The movie tells the story of an ancient warrior.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 고대 전사의 이야기를 그린다.

washbowl.en: a bowl-shaped sink in a bathroom used for washing hands and face

ex.en: He washed his face at the washbowl.
ex.ko: 그는 세면대에서 세수를 했다.

waste.en: to use something in a careless or unnecessary way

ex.en: Don't waste your money on things you don't need.
ex.ko: 필요 없는 것에 돈을 낭비하지 마.

waste.en: material that is thrown away because it is no longer needed

ex.en: The factory produces a lot of waste.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 많은 폐기물을 배출한다.

waste.en: the act of using something in a useless or careless way

ex.en: Watching TV all day is a waste of time.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 TV를 보는 것은 시간 낭비다.

wasteful.en: using more time, money, or resources than necessary

ex.en: It's wasteful to leave the lights on all day.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 불을 켜 두는 것은 낭비다.

waterfall.en: a place where a river falls from a high point to a lower one

ex.en: They took a photo in front of the waterfall.
ex.ko: 그들은 폭포 앞에서 사진을 찍었다.

wax.en: a soft substance made from fat or oil that becomes hard when it cools

ex.en: The candle is made of wax.
ex.ko: 그 초는 밀랍으로 만들어졌다.

wax.en: to put wax on a surface to protect it or make it shine

ex.en: He waxed his car every weekend.
ex.ko: 그는 주말마다 자동차에 왁스칠을 했다.

weakness.en: a part of someone's character or ability that is not good or strong

ex.en: Public speaking is her main weakness.
ex.ko: 대중 앞에서 말하는 것이 그녀의 주된 약점이다.

weakness.cue: 무언가를 유독 좋아하는 성향(a weakness for)
weakness.en: a strong liking for something, even though it may not be good for you

ex.en: He has a weakness for chocolate cake.
ex.ko: 그는 초콜릿 케이크를 유난히 좋아한다.

weapon.en: an object used to hurt, damage, or kill someone

ex.en: The police found a weapon at the scene.
ex.ko: 경찰은 현장에서 무기를 발견했다.

weather forecast.en: a report that predicts what the weather will be like

ex.en: According to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 일기예보에 따르면 내일 비가 올 것이다.

webcam.en: a small camera connected to a computer, used to send video over the internet

ex.en: We had a video call using her webcam.
ex.ko: 우리는 그녀의 웹캠으로 화상 통화를 했다.

weird.en: strange or unusual, in a way that seems surprising or slightly frightening

ex.en: That's a really weird noise coming from the engine.
ex.ko: 엔진에서 정말 이상한 소리가 난다.

well-dressed.en: wearing attractive, neat, and fashionable clothes

ex.en: A well-dressed man walked into the office.
ex.ko: 옷을 잘 차려입은 한 남자가 사무실로 들어왔다.

ex.en: The ship sailed westward across the ocean.
ex.ko: 그 배는 바다를 가로질러 서쪽으로 항해했다.

wetland.en: an area of land that is always wet or covered with water

ex.en: The wetland is home to many rare birds.
ex.ko: 그 습지는 많은 희귀 새들의 서식지이다.

whale.en: a very large sea animal that breathes air through a hole on top of its head

ex.en: We saw whales swimming near the boat.
ex.ko: 우리는 배 근처에서 헤엄치는 고래를 보았다.
